channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple fixes Passwords app security bug with new 18.2 update
item.description: Make sure to update to the latest 18.2 operating system version to patch the Passwords' fix. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: From gut feeling to data-driven strategy: AI’s role in holiday retail success
item.description: Discover how AI transforms holiday retail, replacing guesswork with precise, data-driven customer strategies.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 4 key AI risks to address when contracting services or products
item.description: With the rapid rise of AI, especially GenAI, clients are evaluating risks from partner or vendor use of AI. CIOs and organizations are advised to consider how these risks may impact their operations and security and create contractual terms to address them.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV: Pioneering the future of AI-powered public safety
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Enterprise buyer’s guide: How to choose videoconferencing software
item.description: When most businesspeople think of videoconferencing software, the first thing that comes to mind is probably Microsoft Teams or Zoom, but there are many other choices, each with its own strengths.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google’s AI coding entry with Jules signals tougher competition in coding tools
item.description: Google has made a significant entry into the competitive AI coding tools market with Jules, an experimental coding assistant aimed at enhancing developer workflows and challenging established tools like GitHub Copilot and Amazon Q Developer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Gap Between Business Expectations and IT Reality: A Data Readiness Issue
item.description: A survey by Capital One highlights the disparity between business leader confidence and IT practitioner experiences in AI adoption due to data readiness issues.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AWS adds Graviton Savings Dashboard to help enterprises optimize infra costs
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has launched a dashboard for its Arm-based Graviton CPU that will measure savings opportunities in an effort to help enterprises further optimize their expenditure on subscribed infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google-Backed Character.AI Faces Federal Lawsuit Over Alleged Harm to Minors
item.description: Google-backed Character.AI faces federal lawsuit over alleged harm to minors, including suicide, self-mutilation, and depression.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Google launches Gemini 2.0 AI models, and showcases their powers in new agents
item.description: Google on Wednesday gave the public and developers a taste of the second generation of its Gemini frontier models, and a preview of some of the agents it will power.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: DoD sunsets Task Force Lima, introduces AI rapid capabilities cell
item.description: [Task Force Lima] had made so much progress and had such concrete recommendations that we moved to stand up this cell for actual execution,” said Radha Plumb. The post DoD sunsets Task Force Lima, introduces AI rapid capabilities cell first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: New approach to Rocky Mountain grizzlies sought ahead of the second Trump presidency
item.description: Environmental groups are asking the U.S. government to do more to protect grizzly bears in Wyoming, Montana and Idaho.
item.dc:creator: Mead Gruver, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google claims breakthrough with Willow quantum computing chip but no real-world use yet
item.description: Google’s announcement on Monday of its new quantum chip, called Willow, was full of eye-catching statements. For starters, Willow took less than a minute to perform a benchmark computation that would take one of today’s fastest supercomputers 10 septillion years to do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI app helps you turn anything into LEGO models
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI in the workplace is forcing younger tech workers to rethink their career paths
item.description: Entry-level workers increasingly fear job loss or significant changes to their careers due to automation made possible by generative AI (genAI).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kylie Kelce unseats Joe Rogan as top podcast with debut episode
item.description: Kylie Kelce, wife of NFL player-turned-media personality Jason Kelce, beat out Joe Rogan for the top podcast spot on both Apple and Spotify this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Study: Scientists make more discoveries with help from AI
item.description: A new study by a PhD student at MIT indicates that AI tools can help scientists make more discoveries, according to Nature. In the study by Aidan Toner-Rodgers, an unspecified laboratory in the US with 1,018 researchers used an unspecified custom machine-learning tool.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Setapp survey reveals what Mac developers are thinking about right now
item.description: Mac developers have a lot to think about as we close in on 2025, the eighth Mac developer survey from Setapp suggests.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Boosting Cyprus' defense is key so close to the war-wracked Mideast, defense minister says
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Bolstering Cyprus ' defense capacity is critical for the east Mediterranean island nation so close to the war-wrecked Middle East, the country's defense minister said Tuesday. The priority for Cyprus is to procure better defense systems, said Defense Minister Vasilis Palmas.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Working with PDF files on Linux
item.description: The PDF (portable document format) standard was created in the 1990s by Adobe cofounder John Warnock to make it possible for people to share reliably formatted documents across a wide range of platforms. It became an open ISO standard in 2015.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: IT at Tokio Marine means continuous learning — and many hats
item.description: The insurance industry is known for being slow and steady, but at Tokio Marine North America Services (TMNAS), a dogged emphasis on growth, diversity of thought, and continuous learning has helped accelerate an IT culture predicated on innovation and career mobility.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 5G never delivered for cloud computing
item.description: I recall this as if it was yesterday. I entered the conference room to talk about cloud computing and 5G. I gave this type of presentation often a few years ago. The executives were excited about 5G’s potential. I took a deep breath, knowing I had to voice my concerns.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: HPE execs reveal why they cancelled $700M AI server deal
item.description: There was one particular juicy tidbit during Hewlett Packard Enterprise’s (HPE’s) fourth quarter earnings call with financial analysts last week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: California to consider requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California, home to some of the largest technology companies in the world, would be the first U.S. state to require mental health warning labels on social media sites if lawmakers pass a bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Energy prioritizes information sharing, AI for federated enterprise cybersecurity
item.description: The federated nature of the Energy Department's enterprise requires a focus on information sharing in order to identify and prevent cybersecurity events. The post Energy prioritizes information sharing, AI for federated enterprise cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Protecting yourself from Microsoft tech support scams
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says scammers exploit fears, falsely claim computer issues to access your personal data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why AI coding assistants are best for experienced developers
item.description: Generative AI has hit the mainstream with software developers. According to a recent GitHub survey, more than one million developers actively use GitHub Copilot. More importantly, these developers increasingly use AI as “a new building block in developing applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: IAEA chief: Iran is poised to 'quite dramatically' increase stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium
item.description: MANAMA, Bahrain (AP) — Iran is poised to “quite dramatically” increase its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium as it has started cascades of advanced centrifuges, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency warned Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Don’t scam yourself with the tricks hackers don’t want me to share
item.description: Cybercriminals are tricking people into compromising their own technology by mimicking trusted sources; here's how to keep yourself safe from these "scam-yourself" attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Get ready to build your own Lego masterpieces with this new tech
item.description: Brick My World is an innovative app that allows you to turn any object into a Lego creation with your phone using advanced artificial intelligence and mobile scanning technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China's ban on key high-tech materials could have broad impact on industries, economy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has banned exports of key materials used to make a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to pro
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: From outsider to the Oval Office, bitcoin surges as a new administration embraces crypto
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Born from the ashes of the 2008 global financial crisis, bitcoin arrived after trust had withered in the financial system and in Washington's ability to protect those who must participate in it.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, NewsNation
